author: @giovannip.1433
text: Add some angled mirrors and a water reservoir at height and yo have an ecofriendly generator. The water supply needs to be at height to be able to feed water into the pressurized vessel. A water supply control valve could be used to adjust head pressure to the pressure generated by the heated vessel...

author: @strongforce8466
text:
Very awesome ! I was actually thinking about steam engines the other day and I been meaning to drop a comment to ask if you could look into making a DIY one if it was possible and boom few days later, you post this, incredible, synchronicity :), how about using one of those huge paint canisters of like 10 liter for the bigger brother ?! and of course seal everything maybe even solder it and yeah like some other suggested I'd be curious to see if reducing the output size and adding nozzles like rocket engines could help boost the torque. 

And yeah it could potentially explode, perhaps you should mention that playing with pressure/steam like that could be dangerous, even if it seems obvious, some people might not realize, if you see the thing start inflating.. run lol, although it might just create 1 hole and not explode but you never know, some people might try with different kinds of containers.

Quickly, while I'm at it, I have 2 suggestions to make, I think you should have a link to your main channel in TNT videos and vice versa, in each videos, I have noticed you haven't, some people might discover you through TNT and not know about your main channel unless you mention it.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I wouldn't bother too much about it blowing up mate - it already has two holes in it lol - I like the big can idea seems we think very much along the same lines lol - good advice on  the links - cheers mate

replies.author: @strongforce8466
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering aha true, though I was thinking if the pressure build up amount outpace the output potentially you never know, I was more worried about someone not as knowledgeable as you messing something up or trying too tiny outputs maybe, glad you like the idea and suggestions!! There are more where that came from 😄🔥

replies.author: @TnTOmnibus
replies.text: @strongforce8466 keep 'em coming mate - I love it tbh -on both sides you are right to point it out if I did a higher pressure version chance are I would rob the pressure relief valves from an old pressure cooker both the wighted valve and the emergency blowout grommet  - I know folks read the comments and they can be. a great inspiration and help to folks - it is one of the things I love about this channel the comments section is just plain awesome and full of great ideas

replies.author: @strongforce8466
replies.text: @TnTOmnibus  oh true good idea ! so if the pressure exceeds a certain amount it would just blow the excess out, I'm thinking there might even be some commercially available ones, potentially with a pressure regulator but probably won't be cheap though, would be nice to test different settings of pressure !

author: @Flummizurush
text:
It is Great How You make so simple looking Objects derived from Genius Concepts made from Everyday Stuff, Ingenuity and Creativity!! And then also Videos about it!

When I saw the Engine I, had One Question in Mind, would the Poweroutput change, if it had 3, 4 or 5 Tubes?

Physicist Here
The Engine was going slow, because You had a lot of Water to heat at the same Time and the Steam Pressure couldn't go up..

For the first part You want to flashboil the Water and I had an Idea for it:
Drill a Hole in the Bottom and attach a closed Pipe to the Can. This is going to be the new Heat Chamber - You could also experiment with the Length of this Tube.
Then inside the Can is a second Can attached to the Walls of the outside Can, so that it floats, maybe also adjustable, above the Hole in the Center.

And maybe use thicker Steel for the Outside Can, if You want to weld Stuff to it.

And for the Pressure maybe Valves?

Cheers :)

author: @scotttovey
text:
This functions similar to a helicopter jet I seen in a mag some years back.
Small get engines were attached to the tips of the helicopter rotor which then spun the blade resulting in flight.

Adding pressure relief valves to those tubes so that the pressure has to build up will give you more pressure and result in a faster spin.

But, the spin will likely be intermittent as the pressure goes up and down and too much pressure will overcome whatever mechanism you are using to hold the centered flange onto the can.
